body.title: Document bus+shim interaction in CLAUDE.md and TheoryOfOperation
body.result: Fixed envelope field names in ToO §1.3; added IDLE model + request/response walkthrough; added CLAUDE.md bus+shim section so CC sessions can't mistake RPC as missing
body.description:
CLAUDE.md and TheoryOfOperation §1.3 describe the bus as pub/sub but do not make clear that shims own IDLE and that request/response is already first-class. This caused a misdiagnosis of 'agent-to-agent RPC' as a gap when it already works. CC sessions reading CLAUDE.md at startup need to understand: shims handle IDLE interrupts; agents don't poll; request/response is built-in via reply_to + correlation ID convention; the bus is [REDACTED-CREDENTIAL].

Also add a brief 'what the rack already gives you for free' section to CLAUDE.md so future design conversations start from a correct baseline.

**Affected files:** CLAUDE.md, docs/TheoryOfOperation.md (§1.3 Bus)
**Design rules:** CLAUDE.md is the session-start orientation — architecture facts belong here
**Scope boundary:** IN — bus+shim IDLE model; request/response pattern; what the rack gives for free; OUT — new architecture, code changes
**Completion criteria:** CLAUDE.md contains a clear description of shim-owns-IDLE and request/response; TheoryOfOperation §1.3 updated; a CC session reading both could not mistake RPC as a missing capability
